focus: Audit all live rich description.json files and compare regeneration-preservation strategies against the actual save-path semantics

graphEvents.detail: Current tree contains 28 rich description.json files: 27 implementation, 0 research, 1 archived, so the earlier 29-file reference is slightly stale
Rich live files mostly customize title, with smaller subsets using type, trigger_phrases, and path; only one archived backup adds custom keys importance_tier and short_description
Schema-valid save flow preserves optional and unknown authored fields by spreading normalized existing data before applying canonical overrides

graphEvents.name: true_loss_boundary
graphEvents.detail: Current regen primarily overwrites canonical fields and parse-error files; live rich-field loss risk is more contractual than operational
Field-level merge policy best matches the current code shape because it formalizes authored-field preservation without a schema-model migration
Inspect Phase 018 interaction points and turn the field-level merge recommendation into a concrete implementation contract

name: rich_file_field_counts
detail: title(28), type(12), path(6), trigger_phrases(5), custom-key files(1)

name: rich_file_packet_distribution
detail: implementation rich files dominate (27), research currently uses none, archived contributes one backup-only example

name: true_loss_boundary
detail: Current regen primarily overwrites canonical fields and parse-error files; live rich-field loss risk is more contractual than operational

name: preservation_mechanism
detail: normalizePerFolderDescription starts from the full parsed object and getDescriptionWritePayload merges existing.data before canonical overrides, which is why optional and unknown fields currently survive valid rewrites

name: strategy_rank
detail: Recommend field-level merge policy first; treat schema-versioned authored layer as a heavier follow-on only if Phase 018 requires explicit metadata separation

name: hash_only_primary_strategy
detail: Hash-based change detection helps skip rewrites but does not define canonical-versus-authored ownership semantics
Inspect Phase 018 interaction points and turn the field-level merge recommendation into a concrete implementation contract
